risk_highlights.loss_history_summary: Over 5 loss years: 48 total claims, 1378733.13 total paid, 1510580.11 total incurred; 13 open, 35 closed. Annualized loss ratio 12.9% (avg annual incurred 302116.02 vs. current annual premium 2333947.33). By coverage line — Workers' Compensation: 24 claims, 826622.56 paid, 896295.98 incurred; Commercial Auto: 16 claims, 387376.46 paid, 449550.02 incurred; Commercial Property: 2 claims, 106691.36 paid, 106691.36 incurred; General Liability: 5 claims, 54126.51 paid, 54126.51 incurred; Motor Truck Cargo: 1 claim, 3916.24 paid, 3916.24 incurred; Umbrella/Excess: 0 claims, 0.00 paid, 0.00 incurred. Cross-check: line-level totals sum to 1378733.13 paid and 1510580.11 incurred, consistent with stated totals.

extracted_text_sections.executive_summary: Great Lakes Freight Systems, Inc. is a regional trucking and freight logistics company providing LTL and FTL general freight, warehousing and distribution services across the Midwest and Mid-Atlantic corridors, headquartered in Columbus, OH, with approximately 38 years of operating history. This is a transfer submission to Standard Insurance Company requesting six lines of coverage. Broker recommends competitive pricing based on favorable loss history and proactive risk management posture.
extracted_text_sections.operations_description: Regional trucking and freight logistics — LTL (Less-than-Truckload) and FTL (Full Truckload) general freight, warehousing and distribution services across the Midwest and Mid-Atlantic corridors. Headquartered in Columbus, OH at 1200 Nationwide Boulevard. Operates a fleet of 37 vehicles with 485 employees.
extracted_text_sections.safety_programs: The insured maintains a formal business continuity and disaster recovery plan tested annually. The insured maintains a dedicated safety committee that meets monthly and conducts quarterly site audits. An employee assistance program and workplace wellness initiative have been in place for over five years. The insured carries contractual risk transfer provisions in all subcontractor and vendor agreements. A third-party risk control survey was completed within the past 12 months with favorable results. The insured has implemented a formal return-to-work program that has reduced lost-time claims by over 30%. All locations are equipped with security camera systems and 24/7 monitoring services. With 485 employees and a structured HR program, the insured maintains low turnover and consistent safety training. The insured's fleet of 37 vehicles is maintained under a documented preventive maintenance schedule.
extracted_text_sections.claims_narrative: The loss experience reflects a mixed trend with some large individual claims in the review period. Over the review period (5 loss years), the insured has incurred 48 total claims with 1378733.13 in total paid losses and 1510580.11 total incurred. Currently 13 claims remain open and 35 have been closed. Annualized loss ratio is 12.9% (avg annual incurred 302116.02 against current annual premium 2333947.33 over 5 loss years). Breakdown by coverage line: Workers' Compensation — 24 claims, 826622.56 paid, 896295.98 incurred; General Liability — 5 claims, 54126.51 paid, 54126.51 incurred; Commercial Auto — 16 claims, 387376.46 paid, 449550.02 incurred; Commercial Property — 2 claims, 106691.36 paid, 106691.36 incurred; Umbrella/Excess — 0 claims, 0.00 paid, 0.00 incurred; Motor Truck Cargo — 1 claim, 3916.24 paid, 3916.24 incurred.
